factors.summary: The company faces significant pension and other postretirement benefit funding obligations, which may adversely affect its liquidity, results of operations, and financial condition.

evidence.text: Our pension funding obligations are significant. The amount of our pension funding obligations will depend on the performance of investments held in trust by the pension plans, interest rates for determining liabilities and actuarial experience. We also have significant obligations for retiree medical and other postretirement benefits.
evidence.subsection: We have significant pension and other postretirement benefit funding obligations, which may adversely affect our liquidity, results of operations and financial condition.

factors.summary: The company's financial condition could trigger adverse terms in its credit card processing agreements, including holdbacks on cash, which would reduce liquidity.

evidence.text: These credit card processing companies are not currently entitled to maintain any holdbacks pursuant to these requirements. These holdback requirements can be implemented at the discretion of the credit card processing companies upon the occurrence of specific events, including material adverse changes in our financial condition or the triggering of a liquidity covenant. The imposition of holdback requirements, up to and including 100% of relevant advanced ticket sales, would materially reduce our liquidity.
evidence.subsection: If our financial condition worsens, provisions in our credit card processing and other commercial agreements may adversely affect our liquidity.

factors.summary: Capital expenditures are significant, with a focus on new aircraft purchases, indicating confidence in future growth.

evidence.text: $1.8 billion of capital expenditures...purchases of 11 Boeing 737 MAX aircraft, six Embraer 175 aircraft, etc.
evidence.subsection: Sources and Uses of Cash

factors.summary: Order backlog is substantial, with significant aircraft purchase commitments, indicating strong demand.

evidence.text: definitive purchase agreements for the acquisition of 185 new aircraft... delivery schedule represents our best estimate.
evidence.subsection: Aircraft and Engine Purchase Commitments

factors.summary: The company faces challenges in retaining key personnel due to a competitive job market and economic recovery.

evidence.text: We believe that our future success will depend in large part on our ability to attract, develop and retain highly qualified management, technical and other personnel. Retaining and recruiting people with the appropriate skills is particularly challenging as the economy in general, and the airline industry in particular, continue to recover from the COVID-19 pandemic, resulting in competition for the human resources necessary to operate our business successfully.
evidence.subsection: The loss of key personnel upon whom we depend to operate our business or the inability to attract and develop additional qualified personnel could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: Economic conditions are a headwind due to ongoing recovery from the COVID-19 pandemic and inflationary pressures.

evidence.text: Our business, results of operations and financial condition have been and will continue to be affected by many changing economic and other conditions beyond our control, including global events that affect travel behavior, and our results of operations could be volatile and fluctuate due to seasonality.
evidence.subsection: The loss of key personnel upon whom we depend to operate our business or the inability to attract and develop additional qualified personnel could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: Customer concentration is a risk due to reliance on third-party service providers and regional operators.

evidence.text: A significant portion of our regional operations are conducted by third-party operators on our behalf and are provided for under capacity purchase agreements. Due to our reliance on third parties to provide these essential services, we are subject to the risk of disruptions to their operations, which has in the past and may in the future result from many of the same risk factors disclosed in this report, such as the impact of adverse economic conditions, the inability of third parties to hire or retain skilled personnel, including in particular pilots and mechanics, and other risk factors, such as an out-of-court or bankruptcy restructuring of any of our regional operators.
evidence.subsection: The loss of key personnel upon whom we depend to operate our business or the inability to attract and develop additional qualified personnel could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: Reputation risk is significant due to negative publicity and customer perceptions.

evidence.text: Maintaining a good reputation globally is critical to our business. Our reputation or brand image could be adversely impacted by, among other things, any failure to maintain high ethical, social and environmental sustainability practices for all of our operations and activities, our impact on the environment, public pressure from investors or policy groups to change our policies, such as movements to institute a 'living wage,' customer perceptions of our advertising campaigns, sponsorship arrangements or marketing programs, customer perceptions of our use of social media, or customer perceptions of statements made by us, our employees and executives, agents or other third parties.
evidence.subsection: Any damage to our reputation or brand image could adversely affect our business or financial results.

factors.summary: The company faces potential material litigation exposure that could impact its financial position and liquidity.

evidence.text: We may be a party to litigation in the normal course of business or otherwise, which could affect our financial position and liquidity. Legal proceedings can be complex and take many months, or even years, to reach resolution, with the final outcome depending on a number of variables, some of which are not within our control. If a legal proceeding is resolved against us, it could result in significant compensatory damages, and in certain circumstances punitive or trebled damages, disgorgement of revenue or profits, remedial corporate measures or injunctive relief imposed on us.
evidence.subsection: We may be a party to litigation in the normal course of business or otherwise, which could affect our financial position and liquidity.

factors.summary: The airline industry is highly competitive, with significant competition from low-cost carriers and other forms of transportation, which could negatively impact the company's operations and financial performance.

evidence.text: Our competitors include other major domestic airlines and foreign, regional and new entrant airlines, as well as joint ventures formed by some of these airlines, many of which have greater financial or other resources and/or lower cost structures than ours, as well as other forms of transportation, such as rail and private automobiles or alternatives to commuting or business travel including remote or flexible working policies and communication alternatives such as videoconferencing. In many of our markets, we compete with at least one low-cost carrier (including so-called ultra-low-cost carriers).
evidence.subsection: The airline industry is intensely competitive and dynamic.

factors.summary: Free cash flow is improving due to higher operating revenues and cost management.

evidence.text: At September 30, 2023, AAG had $13.5 billion in total available liquidity and $925 million in restricted cash and short-term investments. Additional detail regarding our available liquidity is provided in the table below (in millions):
evidence.subsection: Liquidity

factors.summary: The company faces significant compliance costs and operational constraints due to environmental regulations.

evidence.text: We are subject to a number of increasingly stringent federal, state, local and foreign laws, regulations and ordinances relating to the protection of human health and the environment and noise reduction...
evidence.subsection: We are subject to many forms of environmental and noise regulation and may incur substantial costs as a result.

factors.summary: Environmental and social factors, particularly climate change, pose significant risks and costs, impacting the company's operations and financial performance.

evidence.text: Efforts to transition to a low-carbon future have increased the focus by global, regional and national regulators on climate change and greenhouse gas (GHG) emissions, including carbon dioxide (CO 2 ) emissions...
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely affected by conflicts overseas or terrorist attacks; the travel industry continues to face ongoing security concerns.

factors.summary: The leisure travel segment is performing better than business travel, affecting yields and revenue.

evidence.text: Passenger yield decreased due to a shift in travel mix towards leisure, which typically has lower yields compared to business travel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Labor costs increased significantly due to higher wages from a new pilot agreement.

evidence.text: Salaries, wages, and benefits increased by $590 million, or 17.4%, primarily driven by higher wage rates associated with the ratification of a new collective bargaining agreement with our mainline pilots.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Productivity improved slightly as load factors increased, but this was offset by lower yields.

evidence.text: Passenger load factor was 84.0% in 2023 compared to 85.3% in 2022, a decrease of 1.3 percentage points. However, CASM excluding net special items and fuel increased slightly.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Capacity utilization increased, as indicated by higher RPMs and ASMs, but load factors declined slightly.

evidence.text: Revenue passenger miles (RPMs) increased by 5.2%, and available seat miles (ASMs) increased by 6.9%, while passenger load factor decreased by 1.3 percentage points.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Management appears cautious due to cost pressures and uncertain economic conditions.

evidence.text: The company remains committed to actively managing its cost structure, which is necessary given the industry's dependence on economic conditions and fuel prices.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Near-term risks include rising labor costs, economic conditions, and fuel price volatility.

evidence.text: The company is exposed to fluctuations in fuel prices and faces challenges in managing labor costs due to the new pilot agreement.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is highly dependent on technology and automated systems, which are critical to its operations.

evidence.text: We are highly dependent on existing and emerging technology and automated systems to operate our business.
evidence.subsection: We rely heavily on technology and automated systems to operate our business, and any failure of these technologies or systems could harm our business, results of operations and financial condition.

factors.summary: The company is working on retrofitting aircraft to prevent interference from 5G signals, which is expected to be completed soon.

evidence.text: American has completed the retrofit of all impacted mainline aircraft and Embraer regional aircraft. Due to supply chain issues, retrofits of the CRJ regional fleet are expected to be completed in the second half of 2023.
evidence.subsection: We rely heavily on technology and automated systems to operate our business, and any failure of these technologies or systems could harm our business, results of operations and financial condition.

factors.summary: The company is investing in cybersecurity measures to address evolving threats.

evidence.text: We react and respond to these cybersecurity attacks in accordance with the applicable legal requirements, our own approved cybersecurity protocols, as well as our commercial partners’ standards.
evidence.subsection: We rely heavily on technology and automated systems to operate our business, and any failure of these technologies or systems could harm our business, results of operations and financial condition.

factors.summary: The company is facing supply chain issues related to retrofitting its CRJ regional fleet.

evidence.text: Due to supply chain issues, retrofits of the CRJ regional fleet are expected to be completed in the second half of 2023.
evidence.subsection: We rely on third-party distribution channels and must manage effectively the costs, rights and functionality of these channels.

factors.summary: Labor relations are under strain, with a new collective bargaining agreement leading to increased costs and ongoing negotiations that could result in adverse effects on operations and financial performance.

evidence.text: a new four-year collective bargaining agreement... significantly increase the cost of providing compensation.
evidence.subsection: Labor Contracts

evidence.text: Union disputes, employee strikes and other labor-related disruptions, or our inability to otherwise maintain labor costs at competitive levels and hire and retain a sufficient number of employees may adversely affect our operations and financial performance.
evidence.subsection: The loss of key personnel upon whom we depend to operate our business or the inability to attract and develop additional qualified personnel could adversely affect our business.

factors.summary: The company's debt leverage remains a significant concern due to high existing indebtedness and substantial obligations, which could limit financial flexibility and increase vulnerability to economic downturns. However, the company is taking steps to deleverage by refinancing and repurchasing debt, which may strengthen its balance sheet.

evidence.text: Our substantial level of existing indebtedness, particularly following the additional liquidity transactions completed in response to the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic; our non-investment grade credit rating; volatile or otherwise unfavorable market conditions; and the availability of assets to use as collateral for loans or other indebtedness, which has been reduced significantly as a result of certain financing transactions we have undertaken since the beginning of 2020 and may be further reduced.
evidence.subsection: We will need to obtain sufficient financing or other capital to operate successfully.

evidence.text: In light of our significant financial commitments...we may continue to take such actions.
evidence.subsection: Capital Raising Activity and Other Possible Actions

factors.summary: Organic revenue growth is mixed, with acceleration in passenger demand and higher yields, but also shows stability and slight increases in total operating revenues.

evidence.text: net cash provided by operating activities was $5.2 billion... return to profitability and net increases in working capital.
evidence.subsection: Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an increase in RPMs, passenger yield and load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Total operating revenues were $13,482 million in 2023 compared to $13,462 million in 2022, a slight increase of 0.1%. Passenger revenue remained flat, while other revenue increased.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is facing rising fuel costs due to inflation and environmental regulations, but has seen a decrease in fuel expenses in recent periods. Despite having some hedging strategies in place, the company's current policy is to not hedge fuel consumption, leaving it exposed to future price fluctuations.

evidence.text: inflationary pressures... increased our costs for... aircraft fuel... hedging arrangements.
evidence.subsection: Certain Covenants

evidence.text: Our current policy is not to enter into transactions to hedge our fuel consumption, although we review this policy from time to time based on market conditions and other factors. Accordingly, as of September 30, 2023, we did not have any fuel hedging contracts outstanding to hedge our fuel consumption.
evidence.subsection: The commercial relationships that we have with other companies, including any related equity investments, may not produce the returns or results we expect.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel and related taxes decreased $1.3 billion, or 12.3%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to a 17.5% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel including related taxes to $2.93 in the first nine months of 2023 from $3.55 in the first nine months of 2022, offset in part by a 6.3% increase in gallons of fuel consumed due to increased capacity.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Concerns over climate change are likely to result in continued attempts by governments to adopt requirements or change business environments related to aviation that, if successful, may result in increased costs to the airline industry and us...
evidence.subsection: We are subject to many forms of environmental and noise regulation and may incur substantial costs as a result.

factors.summary: Input costs are experiencing mixed trends, with fuel costs decreasing due to lower prices but facing upward pressures from inflationary factors such as wages and benefits. Additionally, the company is investing in aircraft retrofits to address 5G interference, which may incur additional costs.

evidence.text: inflationary pressures... increased costs for aircraft fuel, wages and benefits... volatility in certain currencies.
evidence.subsection: Certain Covenants

evidence.text: Market prices for aircraft fuel have fluctuated substantially over the past several years and prices continue to be highly volatile, with market spot prices ranging from a low of approximately $0.37 per gallon to a high of approximately $4.40 per gallon during the period from January 1, 2020 to September 30, 2023.
evidence.subsection: The commercial relationships that we have with other companies, including any related equity investments, may not produce the returns or results we expect.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel and related taxes decreased by $638 million, or 16.6%, due to a 22.0% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Due to supply chain issues, retrofits of the CRJ regional fleet are expected to be completed in the second half of 2023.
evidence.subsection: We rely on third-party distribution channels and must manage effectively the costs, rights and functionality of these channels.

factors.summary: The company's operating margins are facing mixed pressures. While there are signs of expansion due to higher passenger revenue and cost management, there are also significant compressions due to higher labor costs and lower yields. Additionally, margins are under pressure from inflationary costs, with only partial relief from pricing adjustments.

evidence.text: inflationary pressures... increasing the interest expense on our variable-rate indebtedness.
evidence.subsection: Certain Covenants

evidence.text: Total operating revenues in the first nine months of 2023 increased $3.9 billion, or 11.0%, from the first nine months of 2022, driven primarily by the increase in passenger revenue as described above. Our TRASM increased 3.7% to 19.10 cents in the first nine months of 2023 from 18.42 cents in the first nine months of 2022.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Operating income (loss) was $(223) million in 2023 compared to $930 million in 2022, a significant decrease. Pre-tax income also decreased significantly.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company is experiencing positive volume trends, driven by increased passenger demand, higher RPMs, and load factors. However, reliance on third-party distribution channels introduces dependency that could impact these trends.

evidence.text: net cash provided by operating activities was $5.2 billion... return to profitability and net increases in working capital.
evidence.subsection: Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an 8.3% increase in RPMs, a 3.9% increase in passenger yield and an 83.5% load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Revenue passenger miles (RPMs) increased by 5.2%, and available seat miles (ASMs) increased by 6.9%, indicating higher unit volumes.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: We rely on third-party distribution channels... to distribute a significant portion of our airline tickets, and we expect in the future to continue to rely on these channels.
evidence.subsection: We rely on third-party distribution channels and must manage effectively the costs, rights and functionality of these channels.

factors.summary: The revenue mix is showing a mixed trend, with shifts towards higher-margin services and loyalty program contributions in some areas, but also towards lower-margin segments like cargo and international routes in others.

evidence.text: net cash provided by operating activities was $5.2 billion... return to profitability and net increases in working capital.
evidence.subsection: Sources and Uses of Cash

evidence.text: Other operating revenue increased $236 million, or 10.0%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022, driven primarily by higher revenue associated with our loyalty program. During the nine months ended September 30, 2023 and 2022, cash payments from co-branded credit card and other partners were $4.0 billion and $3.4 billion, respectively.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Cargo revenue decreased due to lower cargo yields, while other operating revenue increased, driven by the loyalty program. Passenger revenue remained flat despite higher RPMs.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Regulatory changes, particularly new U.S. tax legislation and climate change-related initiatives, pose significant risks to the company's financial health and operational capabilities. The Inflation Reduction Act introduces a corporate minimum tax and excise taxes on stock repurchases, which could negatively impact future financial condition. Additionally, the International Civil Aviation Organization's CORSIA rules require the company to mitigate CO2 emissions from international flights, adding to operational challenges and costs.

evidence.text: On August 16, 2022, the Inflation Reduction Act (the IRA) was signed into law in the U.S. Among other changes, the IRA introduced a corporate minimum tax on certain corporations with average adjusted financial statement income over a three-tax year period in excess of $1.0 billion and an excise tax on certain stock repurchases by certain covered corporations for taxable years beginning after December 31, 2022. The corporate minimum tax and any excise tax imposed on any repurchases of AAG common stock made after December 31, 2022 may adversely affect our financial condition in the future.
evidence.subsection: New U.S. tax legislation may adversely affect our financial condition, results of operations and cash flows.

evidence.text: The International Civil Aviation Organization (ICAO) has adopted rules, including those pertaining to the Carbon Offsetting and Reduction Scheme for International Aviation (CORSIA), which will require us to mitigate the growth in CO2 emissions associated with a significant majority of our international flights...
evidence.subsection: We may be adversely affected by conflicts overseas or terrorist attacks; the travel industry continues to face ongoing security concerns.

factors.summary: Demand for air travel has shown consistent strength throughout the first nine months of 2023, with notable increases in RPMs and passenger yields, though there were slight decreases in passenger yield during the third quarter.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an 8.3% increase in RPMs, a 3.9% increase in passenger yield and an 83.5% load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Continued strength in demand for air travel in the third quarter of 2023 resulted in a 5.2% increase in revenue passenger miles (RPMs), offset in part by a 4.8% decrease in passenger yield.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: The demand mix has shifted towards leisure travel, which has both positive and negative impacts. While leisure travel has contributed to increased yields and margins, it has also led to lower passenger yields due to the nature of leisure travel typically having lower yields compared to business travel.

evidence.text: The COVID-19 pandemic severely impacted the demand for international travel for a prolonged period, and resulted in the imposition of significant governmental restrictions on commercial air service to or from certain regions. We responded by temporarily suspending a significant portion of our long-haul international flights and delaying the introduction of certain new long-haul international routes.
evidence.subsection: The commercial relationships that we have with other companies, including any related equity investments, may not produce the returns or results we expect.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an increase in RPMs, passenger yield and load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Passenger yield decreased due to a shift in travel mix towards leisure, which typically has lower yields compared to business travel.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: The company's pricing power has shown mixed results. While there has been an increase in passenger yields and RPMs over the nine-month period, there was a decline in passenger and cargo yields over the three-month period.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an 8.3% increase in RPMs, a 3.9% increase in passenger yield and an 83.5% load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Passenger yield was 20.18 cents in 2023 compared to 21.19 cents in 2022, a decrease of 4.8%. Cargo yield also decreased significantly.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

factors.summary: Sensitivities include fuel prices and labor costs, which significantly impact near-term results.

evidence.text: The company is exposed to fluctuations in fuel prices and faces challenges in managing labor costs due to the new pilot agreement.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Evolving cybersecurity and data privacy requirements... could increase our costs, and any significant data security or privacy incident could disrupt our operations, harm our reputation, expose us to legal risks and otherwise materially adversely affect our business, results of operations and financial condition.
evidence.subsection: We rely heavily on technology and automated systems to operate our business, and any failure of these technologies or systems could harm our business, results of operations and financial condition.

factors.summary: The company is implementing cost actions in areas such as fuel expenses and operational efficiency, as well as managing labor costs and distribution channels. However, labor costs remain a significant pressure despite efforts to optimize fuel usage and adjust to new labor agreements.

evidence.text: Aircraft fuel and related taxes decreased $1.3 billion, or 12.3%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to a 17.5% decrease in the average price per gallon of aircraft fuel including related taxes to $2.93 in the first nine months of 2023 from $3.55 in the first nine months of 2022, offset in part by a 6.3% increase in gallons of fuel consumed due to increased capacity.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: The company remains committed to actively managing its cost structure, particularly focusing on fuel costs and labor negotiations.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: To remain competitive, we will need to manage successfully our distribution costs and rights, increase our distribution flexibility and improve the functionality of our distribution channels, while maintaining an industry-competitive cost structure.
evidence.subsection: We rely on third-party distribution channels and must manage effectively the costs, rights and functionality of these channels.

factors.summary: The company experienced mixed performance in capacity, load factors, and revenue per available seat mile. While passenger revenue increased due to stronger demand, load factors declined in the most recent quarter, and RPMs and TRASM showed decreases.

evidence.text: Passenger revenue increased $4.1 billion, or 12.5%, in the first nine months of 2023 from the first nine months of 2022 primarily due to continued strength in demand for air travel, resulting in an 8.3% increase in RPMs, a 3.9% increase in passenger yield and an 83.5% load factor in the first nine months of 2023.
evidence.subsection: Nine Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Nine Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: Available seat miles (ASMs) increased by 6.9%, passenger load factor decreased by 1.3 percentage points, and TRASM decreased by 6.3%.
evidence.subsection: Three Months Ended September 30, 2023 Compared to Three Months Ended September 30, 2022

evidence.text: With retrofits complete or nearly complete, we now expect operational certainty as it pertains to 5G until 2028, when the current operating agreement between the FAA, Federal Communications Commission and telecommunications industry expires.
evidence.subsection: We rely heavily on technology and automated systems to operate our business, and any failure of these technologies or systems could harm our business, results of operations and financial condition.
